mnemonics.title: 2's Complement Steps
mnemonics.content: FLIP (Flip all bits, Add 1) - Remember F-L-I-P: Flip bits, Lightly add 1, Produces negative

mnemonics.title: Overflow Detection
mnemonics.content: SOPS (Sign bits Same, Overflow happens; Different, Subtraction fine) - Overflow when adding two positives or two negatives gives opposite sign

mnemonics.title: Binary to Decimal
mnemonics.content: DOD (Double and Add Digit) - Start with 0, for each bit: double current value and add the bit

acronyms.usage: Representing each decimal digit with 4 binary bits for financial calculations

acronyms.expansion: American Standard Code for Information Interchange
acronyms.usage: 7-bit character encoding for text in computers

acronyms.expansion: Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers
acronyms.usage: Sets floating point standards (754) used in all modern computers
Leftmost bit, indicates sign in signed representations
Rightmost bit, carries least weight in binary number

visualTips.visualization: Think of number line: 0 to 127 positive, -128 to -1 negative wrap around. Adding 1 to 127 wraps to -128. Visualize as a circle or cylinder.
Draw 32-bit box: 1 bit sign (left), 8 bits exponent (middle, shaded), 23 bits mantissa (right). Sign decides positive/negative, exponent scales, mantissa gives precision.
Stack bits vertically. Add from right (LSB) with carry flowing left like water. Write sum and pass carry up.
Only ONE bit changes between consecutive codes. Visualize as a reflection: start with 0,1, then mirror with prefix 0 and 1: 00,01,11,10

analogies.analogy: Like a car's odometer at 999999 going to 000000 (rollover). In signed, going past 127 becomes -128 (wrapping to other side of number line).
Like measuring with a ruler vs caliper. Integer has fixed precision, floating point is like caliper - adjustable 'zoom' based on exponent.
Base-10 is like our 10 fingers, Base-2 is like light switch (on/off), Base-16 is like 16 fingers but using letters A-F for 10-15.

analogies.analization: Think of elevation above/below sea level. Bias is sea level - allows storing negative exponents (below) and positive (above) using only positive stored values.
